patterns.name: Slack bot-to-bot @mention doesn't trigger app_mention
patterns.pattern: Bot @mentions don't trigger other bots' app_mention events. Must also fire webhook. CRITICAL: This rule has been violated 4+ times despite documentation. Agents MUST run slack-notify.ps1 IMMEDIATELY after any Slack send containing @mention. No exceptions. Failure = team dysfunction.

patterns.name: Slack threading rules
patterns.pattern: Thread starter = one short topic line + thread emoji only. All details, @mentions go in thread replies.

patterns.name: Knowledge without checklist = forgotten at action time
patterns.pattern: Rules written but forgotten during action = useless. Critical rules must be in Pre-Action Checklist at top of AGENTS.md, read every session.

patterns.name: Agent no-reply troubleshooting
patterns.pattern: If agent doesn't reply after mention+webhook: check 1) webhook deliver:true 2) port online 3) openclaw.json channel allowlist 4) AGENTS.md integrity 5) session stuck

patterns.name: Slack message.channels event needed for thread mentions
patterns.pattern: app_mention alone doesn't reliably trigger in thread replies. All Slack apps need message.channels event subscription for full thread coverage.

patterns.name: Write other agents files in English only
patterns.pattern: Chinese content written to other agents files via PowerShell can get encoding-corrupted (mojibake). Agent reads garbled lines, skips them silently. Always use English for cross-agent files.

patterns.name: Daily tasks need dedup check
patterns.pattern: Daily tasks (design taste, retro, etc) get re-triggered every heartbeat. Must check heartbeat-state.json or Slack history for today's date before posting. No dedup = duplicate posts.

patterns.name: Never fabricate tool/file operation results
patterns.pattern: NEVER claim a file was copied/created/modified without actual tool output confirming it. If exec/write tool output is missing or unclear, re-run the check. Gatekeeper verifies files on disk. Fabricated deliverables = immediate REJECTED + credibility damage. Rule: always Get-ChildItem after copy/write operations to confirm.

patterns.name: Two-phase ACK then completion
patterns.pattern: For ops that require tool/API execution, respond in two phases: (1) immediate short ACK (received/executing), (2) completion follow-up only after success is confirmed (e.g., API ok:true), including proof (timestamp/link). Improves perceived speed without misreporting completion.

patterns.name: Don't miss non-mention failure messages
patterns.pattern: Non-mention bot failures (e.g., ':warning: Edit failed') and unanswered questions can slip past mention-driven sweeps. After sending any message in an active thread, immediately scan the last ~20 messages for ':warning:'/'failed' keywords and unanswered questions ('?' / common interrogatives) and treat hits as interrupts: react :eyes:, post in-thread status + next step, and assign an owner (mention + webhook if agent).

patterns.name: Never narrate actions ï¿½ execute or stay silent
patterns.pattern: Never post about what you are about to do. Execute the action silently. Narrating 'I will react' or 'let me check' instead of actually doing it is a violation. No internal state updates in Slack.

patterns.name: Monitoring alerts: one top-level per incident, all updates in thread
patterns.pattern: Bot monitoring messages must follow: (1) ONE top-level 'incident starter' per port-down event. (2) ALL subsequent updates (recovery, retries, status changes) go as thread replies to that starter. (3) Deduplicate: same status within N minutes = no new post. (4) Never post duplicate recovery messages at top-level. Violation = channel spam, Xiaosong complaint. Source: 2026-03-02 Creator gateway incident.

patterns.name: Slack emoji reactions: use react.ps1, not slack-notify.ps1
patterns.pattern: slack-notify.ps1 sends text messages only. For emoji reactions use react.ps1: powershell -File C:\Users\azureuser\shared\scripts\react.ps1 -Token <token> -Channel <channel_id> -Timestamp <ts> -Emoji <name>. Never send the word 'react' as a text message.

patterns.name: Stale task (>24h) requires human approval before pickup
patterns.pattern: Never auto-pickup tasks older than 24h from the queue. Silently sitting tasks may be stale, superseded, or abandoned. Verify with Xiaosong before executing. Rule: check file LastWriteTime, if >24h from now -> ask first, do not execute.

patterns.name: Oversight scripts need their own guardrails (syntax-check + staged deploy)
patterns.pattern: Changes to watchdog/automation scripts can silently break monitoring. Guardrail: before any deploy/overwrite of shared/scripts/watchdog.ps1, run syntax-check.ps1 and a dry-run; only then replace. Prefer git commit + rollback path. If watchdog-state.json not updated for >5min during biz hours => alert.

patterns.name: Add-Content on code files = catastrophic corruption
patterns.pattern: NEVER use Add-Content (append) on code files (.ps1, .tsx, .ts, .json, config). Add-Content is for append-only log/diary files only. On code files it creates file bloat, duplication, and syntax errors. Correct approach for code edits: (1) read target block with read tool, (2) use edit tool with exact-match oldText, (3) verify syntax after. Violation: watchdog.ps1 corrupted for 2h on 2026-03-02.

patterns.name: Auto-fix architecture: watchdog owns detection AND fixing
patterns.pattern: Auto-detection and auto-fix must both live in watchdog.ps1 (scheduled task). Detection is independent of agents (agents may be down). Fix logic: (1) deterministic fixes (webhook fire, gateway restart, reminder post) = watchdog does directly; (2) work requiring agent context (retry failed edit) = webhook to that agent; (3) escalate to Xiaosong only if auto-fix fails twice OR judgment/data-loss risk. Log all fix attempts to shared/context/autofix-log.json. Implementation: Invoke-AutoFix function per module. Approved: Xiaosong 2026-03-02.
